name: Valle del Almanzora Greenway Loop
name_original: Minas de Hierro de Almanzora — circular desde Arboleas — Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora
description: Cycle the Valle del Almanzora Greenway Loop, a moderate 17.7-mile route through tunnels, over viaducts, and past historic mining sites.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Arboleas traverse the Almanzora Valley, characterized by olive and citrus groves, orchards, and rolling hills. The region's arid climate has sculpted rugged landscapes in parts, offering diverse visual experiences. Proximity to the Sierra de los Filabres mountain range provides opportunities for more challenging cycling. The area features greenways built on old railway lines, offering accessible routes through varied terrain. Best touring cycling routes around Arboleas The most popular touring cycling route is Valle del Almanzora Greenway Loop, a 17.7 miles (28.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 50 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows an old railway line, featuring tunnels and fertile meadows. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Cantoria Tunnel – Almanzora loop from Los Llanos de Arboleas, a moderate 22.8 miles (36.8 km) path. This loop explores the Almanzora Valley, passing through tunnels and offering scenic views. Local touring cyclists also love the Cantoria Tunnel – Almanzora loop from Arboleas, a 33.3 miles (53.6 km) trail leading through the Almanzora Valley and its tunnels, often completed in about 3 hours 22 minutes. Touring cycling around Arboleas is defined by the fertile Almanzora Valley, arid landscapes, and accessible greenways. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate loops to more difficult routes with significant elevation gains. The routes in Arboleas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Arboleas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Arboleas?
A: There are nearly 60 touring cycling routes in the Arboleas area, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. You'll find everything from easy, mostly paved surfaces to challenging routes with significant elevation gains.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling in Arboleas?
A: Cycling around Arboleas offers diverse scenery. You'll traverse the fertile Almanzora Valley, characterized by olive and citrus groves, orchards, and rolling hills. Parts of the region feature rugged, 'American West-like' landscapes due to the arid climate, and you'll also find yourself close to the more challenging terrain of the Sierra de los Filabres mountain range.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Arboleas offers several easier routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1045672218/valle-del-almanzora-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Almanzora Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 28.5 km trail that follows an old railway line, featuring tunnels and fertile meadows, making it a good option for those seeking a less strenuous ride. There are 4 easy routes in total.

Q: What are the best seasons for touring cycling in Arboleas?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for touring cycling in Arboleas. The weather during these times is ideal for outdoor activities, allowing you to comfortably enjoy the region's diverse landscapes without the extreme heat of summer.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes around Arboleas are circular. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31700542" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cantoria Tunnel – Almanzora loop from Los Llanos de Arboleas&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 36.8 km path that explores the Almanzora Valley. Another option is the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38212706" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cantoria Tunnel – Almanzora loop from Arboleas&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 53.6 km.

Q: What interesting places or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: While cycling, you can encounter traditional *cortijos* (farmhouses) and enjoy cultural stops in towns like Arboleas itself, with its 19th-century Parish Church of Santiago and medieval watchtower ruins. Specific highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5037673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cantoria Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;, and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5312119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bends of the Rambla del Serrón&lt;/a&gt;. You can also explore natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2547310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Bárbara Fountain&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2470738" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turtle Rock&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that follow old railway lines?
A: Yes, the region is known for its greenways built on old railway lines. The Valle del Almanzora Greenway is a significant draw, offering routes that feature railway constructions, tunnels, viaducts, and remnants of the region's mining history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1045672218/valle-del-almanzora-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Almanzora Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great way to experience this.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Arboleas?
A: The touring cycling routes in Arboleas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 80 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from fertile valleys to rugged landscapes, and the accessibility of greenways built on old railway lines.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Arboleas offers 39 difficult routes for experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge. These routes often include significant elevation gains and may feature unpaved segments. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21837741" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arboleas Station – Bends of the Rambla del Serrón loop from La Alfoquia&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 50 km with substantial climbing.

Q: Can I find routes that offer panoramic views of the Almanzora Valley?
A: Yes, many routes provide stunning vistas of the Almanzora Valley. From viewpoints like the Mirador de Limaria, you can enjoy expansive and wonderful views of the valley's olive and citrus groves, orchards, and rolling hills. Routes that traverse the valley often offer these picturesque scenes.

Q: What is the longest difficult route available for touring cycling?
A: For those looking for a longer, more challenging ride, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21839303" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arboleas Station – Bends of the Rambla del Serrón loop from Arboleas&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route spanning approximately 59 km, with over 700 meters of elevation gain.
tips: The Almanzora iron mines were in operation from the end of the 19th century to the middle of the 20th century, and were key in the economic development of the area. Today, it is possible to visit some of the remains of these mines, such as the galleries and tunnels that go into the mountain, and learn more about the mining history of the region.
The Cantoria Iron Bridge is an architectural jewel from the 19th century that crosses the Almanzora River. The bridge has a wrought iron structure and an elegant design.

The Vía Verde crosses the town and invites you to stop to rest, have a drink and enjoy the views of the palace, the old train station and the curious arrangement of its streets in the form of a checkerboard.

faqs.question: How long does it take to cycle the Valle del Almanzora Greenway Loop?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle loop typically takes around 1 hour and 50 minutes to complete. The total distance is approximately 28.5 kilometers (17.7 miles).

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this greenway loop?
faqs.answer: The route features a varied surface, primarily consisting of compacted gravel and asphalt, making it generally accessible. You'll also encounter some unpaved segments. A notable characteristic is its railway heritage, so expect to pass through five tunnels and over several impressive metal viaducts.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Valle del Almanzora Greenway Loop?
faqs.answer: The loop offers diverse scenery, winding through the Almanzora River valley with mountain ranges like Sierra de Baza and Sierra de los Filabres in the background. You'll pass by charming villages, and remnants of the region's industrial past, such as the Iron Mines of Almanzora and the impressive Iron Bridge of Cantoria. The route also goes through the settlement of Almanzora.

faqs.question: Is the Valle del Almanzora Greenway Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While the overall greenway is largely considered suitable for all abilities with many easy sections, this specific loop is rated as moderate. It involves some elevation gain and descent, so a reasonable level of fitness is recommended. Greenways are generally designed to be relatively easy to cycle on hybrid or mountain bikes.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to visit are generally spring or autumn. This helps you avoid the extreme heat of summer and the cold of winter, making for a more comfortable cycling experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Valle del Almanzora Greenway Loop?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details for this exact loop are not provided, but greenways typically have designated access points and parking areas in nearby towns or at trailheads. Look for parking in or around the villages along the route, such as Arboleas, which is a common starting point for sections of the greenway.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route primarily follows sections of the Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora. It also passes through parts of La Jata, Calle el Collejonal, Arroyo Aceituno, Rambla de la Cinta, Rambla de la Jata, and the Antigua linea Murcia-Granada.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Valle del Almanzora Greenway Loop?
faqs.answer: Greenways are generally dog-friendly, but it's always best to keep your dog on a leash to ensure their safety and the comfort of other trail users. Please also remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Valle del Almanzora Greenway Loop. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places to stop for refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: The greenway connects and passes through several charming towns, including Serón, Purchena, Cantoria, and Olula del Río. These villages offer opportunities to explore local culture, find cafes, restaurants, and other amenities for refreshments and breaks.

faqs.question: What kind of bike is best suited for this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the varied surface of compacted gravel, asphalt, and some unpaved sections, a touring bicycle, hybrid bike, or mountain bike would be most suitable for this route. Road bikes with narrow tires might find some sections challenging.
